name: Stir-Fried Tofu or Beef with Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. If using flank steak, cut against the grain into thin slices and marinate with the same ingredients as tofu.
2. Occasionally add a small bok choi, blanching it first and cooling before adding.
3. Increase cornstarch mixture if other vegetables are added; triple the amount listed. The recipe can be doubled and will still turn out well.
4. Prepare tofu according to favorite methods; slice into half-inch blocks, place between paper towels, and press with a telephone book to remove excess water.
5. Mix sherry, salt, pepper, soy sauce, sugar, and 1 1/2 tablespoons of oil in a shallow dish. Add tofu or steak shreds and marinate for 15 minutes.
6. Heat remaining oil in a wok or pan over high heat. Add ginger and onion, stir-frying for 1 1/2 minutes.
7. Remove onions and let drain. Use the oil left in the pan if sufficient.
8. Heat the wok again; add tofu or steak and garlic. Stir-fry until browned.
9. Mix in onion and ginger, then stir-fry for a minute.
10. Stir in cornstarch mixture and cook, stirring for about 30 seconds or until thickened.
